{
  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 90,

  "confidence": 1.0,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Flat or horizontal vent piping installed below the flood level rim of the fixture served."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-route the vent piping so that it rises vertically, or at an angle of not less than 45 degrees from horizontal, to a point at least 6 inches above the flood level rim of the fixture before offsetting horizontally."
  ],
  "rationale": "Both the IPC and UPC prohibit horizontal vent piping below the flood level rim of the fixture because if the drain clogs, waste water can back up into the horizontal vent, deposit solids, and permanently block the vent path once the drain clears.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 905.5",
    "UPC Section 905.3"
  ]
}
